data.narrative:
Antora Energy is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Andrew Ponec and Justin Briggs that has raised $244.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparables universe of nine peers—one public (LNZA) and eight private—all operating in the Climate & Energy sector at similar development stages. The peer set includes Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), among others.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $244.5M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage transitions, implying a valuation of $465M. The peer-median method benchmarks against the six same-sector, same-stage comparables, which have a median valuation of $355M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B.

Triangulating these approaches yields a low estimate of $302M, a median of $410M, and a high of $534M. The peer-median approach anchors the lower bound, while the stage-progression method drives the higher range. The wide dispersion in peer valuations ($120M–$5.0B) reflects material uncertainty around capital intensity, technology readiness, and market adoption timelines within the frontier-energy category. This heterogeneity constrains conviction in any point estimate.
